clauses_raw.clause_text_raw: The purpose of this section is to regulate signage to protect public safety, enhance visual quality, improve legibility, and preserve the heritage character of Charlottetown, especially within the Downtown 500 Lots heritage district.

Signs, and their components, shall not be erected, placed, displayed, altered, or relocated on private or public property unless a sign permit has been issued or the sign is exempted as per the provisions of this section.
Signs, and their components, shall be maintained in a good state of repair so that they do not pose a hazard to public health or safety, or become unsightly.

Normal sign maintenance, including the refurbishment of existing signs, replacement of sign components with identical features, and repair of structural members or lighting shall not require a sign permit.
Signs that do not meet the requirements of this bylaw shall be prohibited unless permitted through a variance.

The sign owner and property owner shall be liable for all signs and parts thereof. The City is indemnified from all claims for damages, injury, loss, or expense arising from the approval, erection, maintenance, removal or falling of a sign, and parts thereof, on private or public property.

sections_raw.section_title_raw: SIGNS PERMITTED WITHOUT A SIGN PERMIT

clauses_raw.clause_text_raw: The following signs are permitted without a sign permit:
Signs identifying the name and/or address of a resident, up to a maximum sign area of 0.2 m2 (2.2 ft2);
Signs regulating the use of a property, such as no trespassing, for rent, or warning signs, up to a maximum sign area of 0.2 m2 (2.2 ft2);
Non-illuminated real estate signs, up to a maximum sign area of:
0.6 m2 (6.5 ft2) in any residential zone or the 500 Lot area;
3 m2 (32.3 ft2) for up to two (2) years in a residential zone when the sign advertises five or more lots in the same subdivision, or a multi-unit dwelling with more than 24 units; and
1.4 m2 (15.1 ft2) in all other zones;

Signs associated with a municipal, provincial, or federal election, up to a maximum sign area of 3 m2 (32.3 ft²). Such signs can only be erected on the day the election period begins and shall be removed within seven (7) days following the election; In the case of a municipal election, the start date is defined to be the Tuesday four (4) weeks prior to the fixed election date.
Signs identifying hazardous or dangerous areas, or the storage of potentially hazardous materials, up to a maximum sign area of 0.6 m2 (6.5 ft2);
One building identification sign denoting the name, number and/or erection date of a building, which has been incorporated into the building's original construction materials;
Signs painted on a mobile canteen;
Any flag, insignia, or bulletin board erected on-site by a charitable organization, religious organization, or a fraternal organization, up to a maximum sign area of 1.7 m2 (18.3 ft2);
Memorial signs or historical plaques denoting the heritage significance of a building, structure, or place;
Signs erected or authorized by a government body (federal, provincial or municipal) including signs placed on properties for rezonings or development agreements as set out in TIS bylaw;
Entrance identification signs placed over, adjacent to, or on a building entrance, including but not limited to a service entrance, sales entrance, or Loading spaces;
Signs placed at the start of a driveway to identify points of access/egress, not including advertising aside from a corporate logo, are permitted on:

Properties in an institutional zone which are 7 acres or greater in lot area, provided that: 1) The maximum sign area is 2.97 m2 (32 ft2); 2) The maximum height is 2.74 m (9 ft); and 3) The sign shall be setback a minimum of 1 m (3.3 ft) from the property line or a building.

Properties in all other zones and in an institutional zone which are less than 7 acres in lot area, provided that: 1) The maximum sign area is 0.5 m2 (5.4 ft2); and 2) The maximum height is 1.22 m (4 ft). 3) The sign shall be setback a minimum of 1 m (3.3 ft) from the property line or a building.

Signs facilitating the movement of pedestrians or vehicles on-site, or denoting the location or function of a building, parking lot, accessible parking, or traffic lane, up to a maximum sign area of 0.37 m2 (4 ft2) and a maximum height or 1.22 m (4 ft);
Non-illuminated window signs provided that the sign covers a maximum of 30% of the window upon which it is attached;
One menu sign, up to a maximum sign area of 0.37 m2 (4 ft2), may be mounted to the facade of an eating and drinking establishment;
Menu signs associated with Drive- through Businesses;
Two construction signs, up to a maximum sign area of 3 m2 (32.3 ft2) and a maximum height of 2 m (6.6 ft);
Banners advertising a grand opening for a business premise outside of residential zones and the downtown zones, provided that:
There are a maximum of two Banners per property;
Banners are a maximum of 2 m2 (21.5 ft2) in area per face;
Banners are securely attached parallel to a building wall, or to a supporting structure(s);
Banners do not extend over a property line, traffic lane, parking space, or an area used for vehicular and pedestrian accessibility; and
Banners are not erected for more than seven (7) consecutive days. Banners exceeding this timeframe shall be subject to Section 4.16; and
Feather banners outside of the 500 Lot Area and residential zones, provided that:
There are a maximum of four feather banners per lot frontage;
All feather banners shall be placed a minimum of 1.83 m (6 ft) apart from each other along the lot frontage;
Feather banners have a maximum width of 0.76 m (2.5 ft) and a maximum height of 3.7 m (12.1 ft) in height;
Feather banners are securely attached to the ground or a solid base;
Feather banners do not extend over a property line, traffic lane, parking space, or an area used for vehicular and pedestrian accessibility; and
Feather banners are not erected for more than 30 consecutive days and 60 days within a calendar year.

A non-illuminated fascia sign, up to a maximum sign area of 0.45 m2 (4.8 ft2), may be erected for a home occupation, home daycare, tourist accommodations, in accordance with the general provisions, and unless the property is a designated heritage resource in which case a Heritage Permit is required.
Signs (including banners) indicating "open", "closed", "sale" or similar terms for any business.
Temporary signs in windows advertising sales and bargains that are displayed for two weeks or less.
Temporary community special event signs that are displayed for the duration of the event and removed within one week of the event's conclusion.
The following signs are strictly prohibited:
Signs posing a hazard to public safety or health, including signs that were previously approved by a Development Officer, but have become hazardous due to inadequate maintenance;
Signs painted on or attached to a fence, utility pole, bench, tree, rock, or any other natural object.
Signs painted on or attached to a parked vehicle or trailer not normally used in the day-to-day activity of a business premise that is visible from a street;
Signs obstructing a window, fire escape, required exit, doorway, or any other building opening intended as a means of ingress or egress;
Signs obstructing the sight triangle area;
Signs, which by reason of location, design, content, colouring, or manner of illumination:
Create a hazard to the safe and efficient movement of vehicular or pedestrian traffic; or
May be confused with, or obstruct the effectiveness of official traffic signs, signals, or devices.
Signs located on a public right-of-way, unless it is erected by a government body or it has been approved by a Development Officer in accordance with this bylaw;
Signs emitting sounds (unless associated with a drive-through business), odours, gases, or liquids;
Signs displaying obscene, offensive, discriminatory, or misleading content;
Abandoned signs;
Billboard signs
Flashing signs, or any sign that incorporates kinetic or pulsating illumination;
Murals or any sign painted upon a building or structure in the 500 Lot Area, unless it has been reviewed by the Arts Advisory Board and approved by Council through the major variance process;
Roof signs
Rotating signs; and
Signs that advertise a product, service, or business that is not available at that location unless otherwise permitted in this bylaw.
Changeable copy signs are not permitted in the 500 Lot Area
A sign that is lawfully in existence at the effective date of this bylaw, although the sign does not conform to the provisions of this bylaw, may continue to be used as a non-conforming sign;
Non-conforming signs may be replaced by an identical sign. A component of a non- conforming sign may also be replaced with an identical component;
The reader board component of a non- conforming sign may be replaced with an electronic sign if it adheres to the bylaw requirements; and
Non-conforming signs shall not be altered, redesigned, or relocated unless:
Alterations comply with this bylaw; or
The total sign area is reduced by a minimum of 25%.
A sign permit application shall contain the following information before a sign permit may be issued:
A completed sign permit application and a paid application fee, in accordance with Council's approved schedule of fees;
Written authorization from, and the contact information of the property owner;

Scaled drawings illustrating the type, dimensions, sign area, colour, finishing material, and supporting structures of all proposed signs and/or sign modifications that are prepared by a qualified professional;

Applications for signs and/or banners that are attached to a wall, shall include elevation drawings of the building wall illustrating the height and length of the wall, placement of windows, location of the proposed sign, and the clearance distance from the ground;
Applications for free standing signs shall include elevation drawings illustrating the sign's design and the clearance between the ground and the sign;

Applications for free standing signs, sandwich board signs, and banners attached to the ground shall include a site plan of the subject property, illustrating the location of existing buildings and structures, property lines, signs, driveways, parking lots, and other site characteristics in relation to property lines. Accurate measurements shall also be provided;
Applications for temporary signs shall include the dates during which the sign will be erected;
Applications for electronic signs shall include a signed letter from the sign manufacturer stating that the sign is equipped to comply with the general provisions of this bylaw; and
Any additional information that a Development Officer or Heritage Officer deems relevant to the issuance of a sign permit for a sign.
A sign permit application shall not be approved when:
The proposed sign does not adhere to the provisions of this bylaw; and
The proposed sign is to be erected on a property containing an existing sign that does not comply with this bylaw;
An issued sign permit shall be valid for one year. If the approved sign is not erected within one year, the permit shall become null and void
A non-complete application will be held for a three (3) month period, at which point the application shall become null and void.
A Development Officer may enter any parcel of land during the City's office hours in order to inspect a sign to determine its compliance with this bylaw;

Where a Development Officer is aware that a sign has been erected prior to the issuance of a sign permit, they will provide notice to the owner, instructing them to submit a completed building permit application immediately;
A Development Officer shall provide notice to the owner of a sign and/or property upon which a sign is placed, instructing them to remove said sign when:
A sign is not permitted, in accordance with the provisions of this bylaw;
A sign and/or property owner has received notice and failed to submit a sign permit application for an illegally erected sign in a reasonable period of time;
A sign, for which a sign permit was previously issued, now violates the conditions of the issued sign permit;
A temporary sign has exceeded the maximum duration specified by this bylaw, the conditions of a sign permit, and/or resolution of Council; and
A sign poses a hazard to public health and safety, has fallen into disrepair, or has become unsightly, as determined by a Development Officer or Bylaw Enforcement Officer.

A residential neighbourhood, multi-unit dwelling exceeding ten units, or farm may have one free standing sign that identifies the entrance and/or name of the building or facility, up to a maximum sign Area of 2.5 m2 (26.9 ft2); and
An industrial park may have one free standing sign that identifies the entrance and/or name of the development, up to a maximum sign area of 3.9 m2 (42 ft2).
The following commercial signs are permitted per business premise, in accordance with the sign dimensions and general provisions outlined in the following tables:
Fascia signs up to a maximum sign area as specified in the table;
A maximum of two additional signs comprised of the following options:
Awning/canopy signs;
Projecting wall sign;
One free standing sign; or
One sandwich board sign.

A permitted business premise in a residential zones, or a legal non-conforming use in a residential zone including the Downtown Neighbourhood (DN) Zone may erect one of the following non-illuminated signs:
A Fascia sign, up to a maximum sign Area of 2 m2 (21.6 ft2); and
A Free Standing sign, up to a maximum sign Area of 2 m2 (21.6 ft2) per sign face and a maximum height of 1.5 m (5 ft).

If the Development Agreement specifically notes any signage requirements that do not meet the requirements of this bylaw, the DA shall supersede this bylaw; otherwise these signage requirements shall be met.
Awning / Canopy Signs shall adhere to the following provisions in Table 9.1:

cells_raw.cell_text_raw: Sign Area shall not exceed 40% of the Awning / Canopy upon which it is attached.

Signs shall be affixed to, or painted on an Awning / Canopy that is securely attached to a building wall that abuts a street; Signs shall be attached to an Awning / Canopy that may extends a maximum of 1 m (3.3 ft) over a sidewalk or public Right-of-way; Signs shall be attached to an Awning / Canopy that is located below the bottom of the second Storey windows; Signs shall be attached to an Awning / Canopy that provides a minimum clearance of 2.2 m (7.2 ft) over a sidewalk and 3 m (9.8 ft) over a Parking Space or traffic lane; The owner of a Sign that extends over a public Right-of-way shall: • Carry liability insurance that names the City as a third party and provides a minimum coverage of $1,000,000; and • Enter into an encroachment agreement with the City to Permit said Sign; and • Signs erected in the 500 Lot Area or on a Heritage Resource shall adhere to the Sign design criteria.
Any other Mixed Use Residential/ Commercial, Institutional, Open Space or Employment/ Industrial Zone
Sign Area shall not exceed 60% of the Awning / Canopy upon which it is attached.

Signs shall be affixed to, or painted on an Awning / Canopy that is securely attached to a building wall that abuts a street; Signs shall be attached to an Awning / Canopy that may extends a maximum of 1 m (3.3 ft) over a sidewalk or public Right-of-way; Signs shall be attached to an Awning / Canopy that is located below the bottom of the second Storey windows; Signs shall be attached to an Awning / Canopy that provides a minimum clearance of 2.2 m (7.2 ft) over a sidewalk and 3 m (9.8 ft) over a Parking Space or traffic lane; The owner of a Sign that extends over a public Right-of-way shall: • Carry liability insurance that names the City as a third party and provides a minimum coverage of $1,000,000; and • Enter into an encroachment agreement with the City to Permit said Sign; and • Signs erected in the 500 Lot Area or on a Heritage Resource shall adhere to the Sign design criteria.

clauses_raw.clause_text_raw: Projecting signs shall adhere to the following provisions in Table 9.2:

cells_raw.cell_text_raw: Sign Area shall not exceed 1 m2 (10.8 ft2) per Sign Face.

Signs shall have a maximum of two parallel Sign Faces; Signs shall be erected on a building wall that abuts a street or the Business Premise's interior parking lot; Signs shall have a minimum clearance of 2.2 m (7.2 ft) from the ground; Signs and their supporting structures shall extend a maximum of 1.1 m (3.6 ft) from the building wall. No Sign shall extend over a side property line or the roof of a building; Supporting structures shall be designed in proportion to the size of the Sign; The owner of a Sign that extends over a public Right-of-way shall carry liability insurance that names the City as a third party and provides a minimum coverage of $1,000,000; and Enter into an encroachment agreement with the City.
Any other Mixed Use Residential/ Commercial, Institutional, Open Space or Employment/ Industrial Zone
Sign Area shall not exceed 2 m2 (21.5 ft2) per Sign Face.

Signs shall have a maximum of two parallel Sign Faces; Signs shall be erected on a building wall that abuts a street or the Business Premise's interior parking lot; Signs shall have a minimum clearance of 2.2 m (7.2 ft) from the ground; Signs and their supporting structures shall extend a maximum of 1.1 m (3.6 ft) from the building wall. No Sign shall extend over a side property line or the roof of a building; Supporting structures shall be designed in proportion to the size of the Sign; The owner of a Sign that extends over a public Right-of-way shall carry liability insurance that names the City as a third party and provides a minimum coverage of $1,000,000; and Enter into an encroachment agreement with the City.
When erected on a designated heritage resource, Sign Area shall not exceed 1 m2 (10.8 ft2) per sign face.

Signs shall have a maximum of two parallel Sign Faces; Signs shall be erected on a building wall that abuts a street or the Business Premise's interior parking lot; Signs shall have a minimum clearance of 2.2 m (7.2 ft) from the ground; Signs and their supporting structures shall extend a maximum of 1.1 m (3.6 ft) from the building wall. No Sign shall extend over a side property line or the roof of a building; Supporting structures shall be designed in proportion to the size of the Sign; The owner of a Sign that extends over a public Right-of-way shall carry liability insurance that names the City as a third party and provides a minimum coverage of $1,000,000; and Enter into an encroachment agreement with the City.

clauses_raw.clause_text_raw: In a multi-tenant building where business premises are clearly divided and have a usable ground-floor storefront, sign area shall be calculated using the length of the building wall occupied by the subject business premise;

Illuminated window signs and non- illuminated window signs covering more than 30% of the window upon which they are attached shall be regulated as fascia signs and occupy part of the permitted sign area; and
In a multi-tenant building where business premises are not clearly divided and lack a usable ground-floor storefront, sign area shall be calculated for the entire building. In

Fascia signs shall adhere to the following provisions in Table 9.3. Sign Area shall not exceed 9.29 m2 (100 ft2) per Sign Face. Signs shall not exceed 8 m in Height. Shopping Centres: Sign Area shall not exceed 30 m2 (323 ft2) per Sign Face. Signs shall not exceed 9.75 m (32.0 ft) in Height. Non-Shopping Centres: Sign Area shall not exceed 13.9 m2 (150 ft2) per Sign Face. Signs shall not exceed 8 m (26.2 ft) in Height. Sign Area shall not exceed 2.32 m2 (25 ft2) per Sign Face. Signs shall not exceed 2.5 m (8.2 ft) in Height. Sign Area shall not exceed 3.72 m2 (25 ft2) per Sign Face. Signs shall not exceed 3.7 m (12.1 ft) in Height. PPS POS Sign Area shall not exceed 4.65 m2 (50 ft2) per Sign Face. Signs shall not exceed 3.7m (12.1 ft) in Height.

A property owner may erect a free standing sign that contains advertising for a business premise(s) that is located on the subject property. A free standing sign that contains advertising for multiple tenants shall contribute to the total number of permitted signs for each business premise listed on said sign;

When a free standing sign shall be used to advertise multiple business premises a Development Officer shall calculate the permitted sign area for the entire property, and the property owner(s) shall determine which usable sign area is allocated to each Signs shall have a maximum of two parallel Sign Faces; Signs shall not impede pedestrian or vehicular visibility when accessing a lot; Signs shall be setback a minimum of 1 m (3.3 ft) from the property line and a building; Signs erected on a corner lot shall be prohibited within the Sight Triangle Area; Signs shall have a minimum clearance of 2.2 m (7.2 ft) above open areas and 4 m (13 ft) above a driveway or vehicular traffic area; When a Business Premise(s) is located on a corner lot or through lot, one Sign is permitted on each of two lot frontages, provided that the second Sign is 50% of the total Sign Area identified in this table, and there is a minimum distance of 30.1 m (99 ft) between the Signs. business premise.

Free standing signs shall be adhere to the following provisions in Table 9.4: Electronic Signs may occupy a maximum of 2.23 m2 (24 ft2) of the permitted Sign Area for Fascia Signs or Free Standing Signs. Electronic Signs may occupy a maximum of 2.79 m2 (30 ft2) of the permitted Sign Area for Fascia Signs or Free Standing Signs.
Electronic signs shall be incorporated into a free standing sign or be erected on a building wall.

Electronic signs shall occupy a portion of the permitted sign area for fascia signs (Section 5.12.4) or free standing signs (Section 5.13.3), as determined by its classification; One Electronic Sign shall be permitted per property. When the subject property is a corner lot or through lot, one Electronic Sign shall be permitted on each of the two lot frontages; Electronic Signs may have two parallel Sign Faces; Electronic Signs shall not be erected within 30.5 m (100 ft) of a residential Zone or the Downtown Neighbourhood (DN) Zone; If installed on a Free Standing Sign, an Electronic Sign shall not be located within 9.14 m (30 ft) of the outermost portion of an intersection roundabout, or interchange. On undersized lots where this figure cannot be satisfied, Signs shall instead be erected at the midpoint of the property lot frontage; If installed on a legal non-conforming Free Standing Sign, Section 47.4.3 shall apply; Signs shall be equipped with automatic dimming technology to automatically adjust Sign brightness in correlation with ambient light conditions. Sign brightness shall not be brighter than 0.3 Foot Candles (equivalent to 0.3 lumen per ft2 or 3.58 lux) above ambient light conditions; Messages shall have a minimum duration of 10 seconds, and shall not include off-premise advertising; The intensity of illumination of a message shall be maintained at a constant level throughout the duration of the message; The transition period between messages shall be a maximum of 0.25 seconds. The message transition shall not incorporate blinking, flashing, scrolling, bouncing, or other moving effects; and Electronic Signs may include static images, however, animation, video, or moving images shall be prohibited.
Electronic signs shall not be permitted:
In the 500 Lot Area;
On a heritage resource; or
In a residential zone; and
Electronic signs shall adhere to the following provisions, in addition to the provisions for fascia signs or free standing signs, as determined by its classification:
Sandwich board signs shall be adhere to the following provisions in Table 9.6:

Sign Area shall not exceed 0.6 m2 (6.5 ft2) per Sign Face. Signs shall not exceed 1m (3.3 ft) in Height. Placed On Public Right-of-way: Sign Area shall not exceed 0.6 m2 (6.5 ft2) per Sign Face. Signs shall not exceed 1 m (3.3 ft) in Height. Placed On Private Property: Sign Area shall not exceed 1.2 m2 (13 ft2) per Sign Face. Signs shall not exceed 1.21 m (4 ft) in Height. Signs shall only be displayed when the advertised Business Premise is open; Signs shall not interfere with pedestrian or vehicular circulation, or impede pedestrian or vehicular visibility when accessing the lot; Signs shall be placed on private property or on the public Right-of-way abutting the subject building, excluding the sidewalk, where possible; Where there is insufficient space to satisfy c., the sign may be placed on the sidewalk abutting the subject building or the outermost edge of the sidewalk, as long as a minimum pathway of 1.5 m (5 ft) exists on the sidewalk; Signs shall display the City's approval sticker indicating that said sign has been approved in accordance with this bylaw; When placed on a public Right-of-way, the owner of a sign shall carry liability insurance that names the City as a third party and provides a minimum coverage of $1,000,000; and Proof of liability insurance shall be provided on an annual basis.
Banners may be permitted on a temporary basis if they adhere to the following provisions in Table 9.7:

Sign Area shall not exceed 1.95 m2 (21 ft2) per Banner face. In the 500 Lot Area, two Banners are permitted per property. In all other areas a maximum of four Banners are permitted per property; Banners shall be securely attached parallel to a building wall, or to a supporting structure(s); Banners shall not extend over a property line, traffic lane, parking space, or an area used for vehicular and pedestrian accessibility; and Banners shall not be erected for more than 30 consecutive days and 60 days within a calendar year. Sign Area shall not exceed 0.30 m2 (3.3 ft2) per linear meter (1.0 ft2 linear foot) of the structure roof upon which the Sign is erected.
Roof signs may only be permitted on floating structures if they adhere to the following provisions in Table 9.8.
Miscellaneous signs, including ribbons, inflatable signs, balloons, activated devices, may be permitted on a temporary basis if they adhere to the following provisions:
Temporary signs shall be prohibited in the 500 Lot Area, unless associated with a special event or festival;
A maximum of two signs are permitted per property;
Signs shall not extend over a property line, traffic lane, parking space, or other area used for vehicular and pedestrian accessibility; and
Signs shall be erected for a maximum of 14 consecutive days and 30 days within a calendar year.

The City has designated locations where off-premise signs for special events may be permitted on a temporary basis. Temporary, off-premise signs for special events shall adhere to the following provisions:

A maximum of six (6) signs shall be erected per special event. The maximum sign area Only permitted on floating structures; Only one (1) roof sign per structure; Roof signs may be attached to the sloping portions of the roof, with no portion of the sign shall be located above the highest point of the roof surface, such as the top of the gable, hip, gambrel or peak portion of the roof; In instances where a structure has a roof sign that same structure may only have one (1) additional fascia sign, subject to the regulations therein. shall not exceed 3 m2 (32 ft2) per sign face;
Signs shall not be illuminated;
Signs shall only be erected at a location designated by the City;

Signs shall be erected for a maximum period of 21 consecutive days and be removed within two days following the events' conclusion. Signs exceeding this maximum duration may be permitted pending Council's approval;
A special event requiring signage on an annual basis may seek Council's permission to erect signs annually, provided that a sign permit application is submitted each year.
Off-site electronic signage, promoting local businesses and local events, may be permitted subject to the following criteria:
The permitted off-premise electronic sign types include 'pole signs' and 'building wall signs' with a maximum sign area of 5 m2 (53.8 ft2).
Off-site electronic signs shall meet the following siting criteria:
Shall not be located within any public street right-of-ways;
Shall be located on lands fronting on collector roads (Schedule C) that are zoned EG, GC, GN, BP, AP, HI or I;
Shall not impede the visibility of any traffic signals or traffic signage;
Shall be located in a manner that does not create distractions or safety hazards for pedestrians or motorists, or a danger to the public; and
Any additional criteria which the City staff believe may pose a safety threat.
Off-site electronic signs shall meet the following setback and design requirements:
60 m from any intersection measured from the closest streetline(s);
100 m from any crosswalk or traffic signal;
At least 15 m from any residential or conservation zones;
At least 2 m from any streetline;
The minimum underside height of any free standing pole type sign shall be 2.2 m; and
The maximum height of any free standing pole type sign shall be 5m.
Off-site electronic signs shall meet the following illumination criteria:
Message transition shall be instantaneous and shall not involve any visible effects including scrolling, fading, dissolving, intermittent or flashing light, or the illusion of such effects;
The minimum dwell time of each image shall be 10 seconds;
The maximum brightness levels shall be 5,000 nits during daytime and 500 nits at nighttime; and
The sign shall not be Illuminated between the hours of 11:00 p.m. and 7:00 a.m.

sections_raw.section_title_raw: SIGN DESIGN CRITERIA - DESIGNATED HERITAGE RESOURCES AND WITHIN THE 500 LOT AREA

clauses_raw.clause_text_raw: Excluding sandwich board signs and temporary signs, all signs that require a sign permit shall adhere to the following design criteria if erected in the 500 Lot Area or on a designated heritage resource:
Content:
Sign content shall be limited to the name, logo(s), or slogan of a business premise, the name of practitioners of said business premise, the name of the building, or the civic address;
Signs shall utilize a maximum of two fonts;
Signs shall be limited to a maximum of three colours, unless incorporated into a corporate logo, with sufficient contrast so that the sign is clearly legible;
Sign board and background colours shall be dark with a light contrast colour for letter detailing; and
Signs shall not have a reflective or neon/ fluorescent background.
Materials & Design:
Signs and channel letters shall be designed in a manner that is consistent with the architectural style and original facade materials of the building upon which they are erected;
Signs and channel letters shall be constructed of natural materials, such as wood, metal, glass, or fabric, or a high quality composite material that appears to be a natural material; and
Crafted signs with three-dimensional features and decorative detailing are encouraged.
Compatibility & Placement:

Signs shall not obstruct any prominent architectural feature or character defining elements of a building, including but not limited to windows, sills, entrances, door frames, arches, columns, cornices, and molding;
Where multiple signs exist for a single business premise, all signs shall incorporate unifying styles, materials, fonts, colours, and/or typologies;
Repetitious sign information on the same building wall shall be prohibited;
Where a sign band exists on a facade, fascia signs shall be centred in the sign band. Where no sign band exists, the fascia sign shall be centred over the doorway or display windows;
Fascia signs that run the full width of the building wall are preferred; and
Signs shall only be located on an awning/canopy that extends the full width of the business premise.
Method of Attachment:

Signs shall be securely mounted onto a structure in a manner that does not have an adverse impact on the building's structural integrity, or compromise its heritage value or character defining elements; and
All fastening hardware and brackets shall blend into, and be compatible with the sign's materials and colours.
Lighting:
Back-lit sign boxes shall not be permitted. Back-lit signs that illuminate only text and logo(s), and have an opaque background, shall be permitted;
Signs that incorporate Halo Lighting or a LED back-lighting that illuminates individual letters shall be permitted;
Light sources or fixtures shall be compatible with the character of the building upon which they're attached;

Light sources or fixtures shall be positioned directly above, below, or to the side of the sign that it illuminates. Lighting shall be shield or directed onto the sign so that it does not shine onto the street or adjacent properties;
Lighting may instead be provided from a light source / fixture that illuminates the sign face(s); and
Light sources shall not extend more than 0.6 m (2 ft) from the building wall upon which they are attached.

Heritage Board shall make a recommendation to Council on sign permit applications for Heritage Designated Properties that do not adhere to the sign design criteria and/or the placement of the sign, but not allow an increase in permitted sign face area.
Planning Board shall review applications and make a formal recommendation to Council for applications:
For off-premise signs for special events where there is a request to erect signs that exceed the maximum duration;
For off-premise signs for special events where there is a request to erect signs on an annual basis; and
For off-premise electronic signs.

map_references_raw.text_raw: Current zoning map raster package represented in PostGIS-compatible spatial artifacts.

requirements.requirement_text_raw: Dimensions Sign Area shall not exceed 40% of the Awning / Canopy upon which it is attached.

General Provisions Signs shall be affixed to, or painted on an Awning / Canopy that is securely attached to a building wall that abuts a street; Signs shall be attached to an Awning / Canopy that may extends a maximum of 1 m (3.3 ft) over a sidewalk or public Right-of-way; Signs shall be attached to an Awning / Canopy that is located below the bottom of the second Storey windows; Signs shall be attached to an Awning / Canopy that provides a minimum clearance of 2.2 m (7.2 ft) over a sidewalk and 3 m (9.8 ft) over a Parking Space or traffic lane; The owner of a Sign that extends over a public Right-of-way shall: • Carry liability insurance that names the City as a third party and provides a minimum coverage of $1,000,000; and • Enter into an encroachment agreement with the City to Permit said Sign; and • Signs erected in the 500 Lot Area or on a Heritage Resource shall adhere to the Sign design criteria.
Dimensions Sign Area shall not exceed 60% of the Awning / Canopy upon which it is attached.

General Provisions Signs shall be affixed to, or painted on an Awning / Canopy that is securely attached to a building wall that abuts a street; Signs shall be attached to an Awning / Canopy that may extends a maximum of 1 m (3.3 ft) over a sidewalk or public Right-of-way; Signs shall be attached to an Awning / Canopy that is located below the bottom of the second Storey windows; Signs shall be attached to an Awning / Canopy that provides a minimum clearance of 2.2 m (7.2 ft) over a sidewalk and 3 m (9.8 ft) over a Parking Space or traffic lane; The owner of a Sign that extends over a public Right-of-way shall: • Carry liability insurance that names the City as a third party and provides a minimum coverage of $1,000,000; and • Enter into an encroachment agreement with the City to Permit said Sign; and • Signs erected in the 500 Lot Area or on a Heritage Resource shall adhere to the Sign design criteria.
Dimensions Sign Area shall not exceed 1 m2 (10.8 ft2) per Sign Face.

General Provisions Signs shall have a maximum of two parallel Sign Faces; Signs shall be erected on a building wall that abuts a street or the Business Premise's interior parking lot; Signs shall have a minimum clearance of 2.2 m (7.2 ft) from the ground; Signs and their supporting structures shall extend a maximum of 1.1 m (3.6 ft) from the building wall. No Sign shall extend over a side property line or the roof of a building; Supporting structures shall be designed in proportion to the size of the Sign; The owner of a Sign that extends over a public Right-of-way shall carry liability insurance that names the City as a third party and provides a minimum coverage of $1,000,000; and Enter into an encroachment agreement with the City.
Dimensions Sign Area shall not exceed 2 m2 (21.5 ft2) per Sign Face.

General Provisions Signs shall have a maximum of two parallel Sign Faces; Signs shall be erected on a building wall that abuts a street or the Business Premise's interior parking lot; Signs shall have a minimum clearance of 2.2 m (7.2 ft) from the ground; Signs and their supporting structures shall extend a maximum of 1.1 m (3.6 ft) from the building wall. No Sign shall extend over a side property line or the roof of a building; Supporting structures shall be designed in proportion to the size of the Sign; The owner of a Sign that extends over a public Right-of-way shall carry liability insurance that names the City as a third party and provides a minimum coverage of $1,000,000; and Enter into an encroachment agreement with the City.
Dimensions When erected on a designated heritage resource, Sign Area shall not exceed 1 m2 (10.8 ft2) per sign face.

General Provisions Signs shall have a maximum of two parallel Sign Faces; Signs shall be erected on a building wall that abuts a street or the Business Premise's interior parking lot; Signs shall have a minimum clearance of 2.2 m (7.2 ft) from the ground; Signs and their supporting structures shall extend a maximum of 1.1 m (3.6 ft) from the building wall. No Sign shall extend over a side property line or the roof of a building; Supporting structures shall be designed in proportion to the size of the Sign; The owner of a Sign that extends over a public Right-of-way shall carry liability insurance that names the City as a third party and provides a minimum coverage of $1,000,000; and Enter into an encroachment agreement with the City.

Properties in an institutional zone which are 7 acres or greater in lot area, provided that: 1) The maximum sign area is 2.97 m2 (32 ft2); 2) The maximum height is 2.74 m (9 ft); and 3) The sign shall be setback a minimum of 1 m (3.3 ft) from the property line or a building.

Properties in all other zones and in an institutional zone which are less than 7 acres in lot area, provided that: 1) The maximum sign area is 0.5 m2 (5.4 ft2); and 2) The maximum height is 1.22 m (4 ft). 3) The sign shall be setback a minimum of 1 m (3.3 ft) from the property line or a building.

Signs facilitating the movement of pedestrians or vehicles on-site, or denoting the location or function of a building, parking lot, accessible parking, or traffic lane, up to a maximum sign area of 0.37 m2 (4 ft2) and a maximum height or 1.22 m (4 ft);
Two construction signs, up to a maximum sign area of 3 m2 (32.3 ft2) and a maximum height of 2 m (6.6 ft);
All feather banners shall be placed a minimum of 1.83 m (6 ft) apart from each other along the lot frontage;
Feather banners have a maximum width of 0.76 m (2.5 ft) and a maximum height of 3.7 m (12.1 ft) in height;
A Free Standing sign, up to a maximum sign Area of 2 m2 (21.6 ft2) per sign face and a maximum height of 1.5 m (5 ft).

Fascia signs shall adhere to the following provisions in Table 9.3. Sign Area shall not exceed 9.29 m2 (100 ft2) per Sign Face. Signs shall not exceed 8 m in Height. Shopping Centres: Sign Area shall not exceed 30 m2 (323 ft2) per Sign Face. Signs shall not exceed 9.75 m (32.0 ft) in Height. Non-Shopping Centres: Sign Area shall not exceed 13.9 m2 (150 ft2) per Sign Face. Signs shall not exceed 8 m (26.2 ft) in Height. Sign Area shall not exceed 2.32 m2 (25 ft2) per Sign Face. Signs shall not exceed 2.5 m (8.2 ft) in Height. Sign Area shall not exceed 3.72 m2 (25 ft2) per Sign Face. Signs shall not exceed 3.7 m (12.1 ft) in Height. PPS POS Sign Area shall not exceed 4.65 m2 (50 ft2) per Sign Face. Signs shall not exceed 3.7m (12.1 ft) in Height.

When a free standing sign shall be used to advertise multiple business premises a Development Officer shall calculate the permitted sign area for the entire property, and the property owner(s) shall determine which usable sign area is allocated to each Signs shall have a maximum of two parallel Sign Faces; Signs shall not impede pedestrian or vehicular visibility when accessing a lot; Signs shall be setback a minimum of 1 m (3.3 ft) from the property line and a building; Signs erected on a corner lot shall be prohibited within the Sight Triangle Area; Signs shall have a minimum clearance of 2.2 m (7.2 ft) above open areas and 4 m (13 ft) above a driveway or vehicular traffic area; When a Business Premise(s) is located on a corner lot or through lot, one Sign is permitted on each of two lot frontages, provided that the second Sign is 50% of the total Sign Area identified in this table, and there is a minimum distance of 30.1 m (99 ft) between the Signs. business premise.

Electronic signs shall occupy a portion of the permitted sign area for fascia signs (Section 5.12.4) or free standing signs (Section 5.13.3), as determined by its classification; One Electronic Sign shall be permitted per property. When the subject property is a corner lot or through lot, one Electronic Sign shall be permitted on each of the two lot frontages; Electronic Signs may have two parallel Sign Faces; Electronic Signs shall not be erected within 30.5 m (100 ft) of a residential Zone or the Downtown Neighbourhood (DN) Zone; If installed on a Free Standing Sign, an Electronic Sign shall not be located within 9.14 m (30 ft) of the outermost portion of an intersection roundabout, or interchange. On undersized lots where this figure cannot be satisfied, Signs shall instead be erected at the midpoint of the property lot frontage; If installed on a legal non-conforming Free Standing Sign, Section 47.4.3 shall apply; Signs shall be equipped with automatic dimming technology to automatically adjust Sign brightness in correlation with ambient light conditions. Sign brightness shall not be brighter than 0.3 Foot Candles (equivalent to 0.3 lumen per ft2 or 3.58 lux) above ambient light conditions; Messages shall have a minimum duration of 10 seconds, and shall not include off-premise advertising; The intensity of illumination of a message shall be maintained at a constant level throughout the duration of the message; The transition period between messages shall be a maximum of 0.25 seconds. The message transition shall not incorporate blinking, flashing, scrolling, bouncing, or other moving effects; and Electronic Signs may include static images, however, animation, video, or moving images shall be prohibited.

Sign Area shall not exceed 0.6 m2 (6.5 ft2) per Sign Face. Signs shall not exceed 1m (3.3 ft) in Height. Placed On Public Right-of-way: Sign Area shall not exceed 0.6 m2 (6.5 ft2) per Sign Face. Signs shall not exceed 1 m (3.3 ft) in Height. Placed On Private Property: Sign Area shall not exceed 1.2 m2 (13 ft2) per Sign Face. Signs shall not exceed 1.21 m (4 ft) in Height. Signs shall only be displayed when the advertised Business Premise is open; Signs shall not interfere with pedestrian or vehicular circulation, or impede pedestrian or vehicular visibility when accessing the lot; Signs shall be placed on private property or on the public Right-of-way abutting the subject building, excluding the sidewalk, where possible; Where there is insufficient space to satisfy c., the sign may be placed on the sidewalk abutting the subject building or the outermost edge of the sidewalk, as long as a minimum pathway of 1.5 m (5 ft) exists on the sidewalk; Signs shall display the City's approval sticker indicating that said sign has been approved in accordance with this bylaw; When placed on a public Right-of-way, the owner of a sign shall carry liability insurance that names the City as a third party and provides a minimum coverage of $1,000,000; and Proof of liability insurance shall be provided on an annual basis.
The minimum underside height of any free standing pole type sign shall be 2.2 m; and
The maximum height of any free standing pole type sign shall be 5m.

other_requirements.requirement_text_raw: 3 m2 (32.3 ft2) for up to two (2) years in a residential zone when the sign advertises five or more lots in the same subdivision, or a multi-unit dwelling with more than 24 units; and
60 m from any intersection measured from the closest streetline(s);
100 m from any crosswalk or traffic signal;
At least 15 m from any residential or conservation zones;
At least 2 m from any streetline;
Light sources shall not extend more than 0.6 m (2 ft) from the building wall upon which they are attached.
